title: Known-issues sprint publish review (workarounds + 'when to escalate')
preview: Internal publish-review for the Known-Issues Sprint. Team did a rapid pass over five workaround articles (streaming disconnects/timeouts, 429 quota mismatch, Dedicated autoscaling oscillation, Private installer upgrade stuck at migrations, audit log delivery latency) to ensure they are copy/paste-ready for Support, ...
text: Internal publish-review for the Known-Issues Sprint. Team did a rapid pass over five workaround articles (streaming disconnects/timeouts, 429 quota mismatch, Dedicated autoscaling oscillation, Private installer upgrade stuck at migrations, audit log delivery latency) to ensure they are copy/paste-ready for Support, include clear escalation criteria, and avoid customer-sensitive details. Agreed on a consistent 'When to escalate' rubric mapped to Support P0/P1 triggers, added owners/reviewers per article, and aligned on what must be linked from Support macros and the known-issues index before merging docs PRs.

title: Support + SRE playbook alignment (escalation triggers, bridges, update cadence)
preview: Support leadership and SRE aligned on (1) what constitutes P0/P1 from Support vs Sev0/Sev1 in incident terms, (2) concrete paging/escalation triggers (error-rate/latency/customer-count/revenue risk), (3) Slack bridge conventions and when to move from #support bridge into #incidents, and (4) update cadence language f...
text: Support leadership and SRE aligned on (1) what constitutes P0/P1 from Support vs Sev0/Sev1 in incident terms, (2) concrete paging/escalation triggers (error-rate/latency/customer-count/revenue risk), (3) Slack bridge conventions and when to move from #support bridge into #incidents, and (4) update cadence language for enterprise vs non-enterprise (and how to handle “Waiting on Customer” without gaming the SLA). The group agreed to standardize a short “page template” (request IDs, region, model, recent deploys, customer tier, and what changed), require linking the SUP Jira ticket in the bridge, and to use a consistent cadence phrased as targets rather than guarantees. Follow-ups include edits to the Confluence SLA table wording, Jira automation for required fields + SLA timers, and incident-bot improvements to pin SUP ticket links in bridge channels.

title: Vertex LegalTech - audit log latency escalation (delivery lag + interim mitigation)
preview: Vertex escalated that audit logs in their Redwood Private/VPC deployment are arriving 30–45 minutes late, causing gaps in their compliance monitoring and alerting. Redwood confirmed the audit-log pipeline can backlog under specific load patterns and that there is currently no customer-visible lag metric/alert. The t...
text: Vertex escalated that audit logs in their Redwood Private/VPC deployment are arriving 30–45 minutes late, causing gaps in their compliance monitoring and alerting. Redwood confirmed the audit-log pipeline can backlog under specific load patterns and that there is currently no customer-visible lag metric/alert. The team aligned on expectations (targeting single-digit minutes, with defined thresholds by tier), immediate mitigations (widen export window, implement retries/backoff, avoid treating the feed as real-time until fixed), and an engineering plan tied to SUP-1846 / ENG-4824 to add a first-class lag metric + alerting + runbook updates. Rachel owned customer comms cadence and a written mitigation note; SRE owned confirming current backlog status and a timeline for the alert rollout.

title: Helix Health - Private installer upgrade blocker triage (stuck at migrations)
preview: Helix Health reported a blocked Redwood Private upgrade where the installer progresses but hangs/fails at the database migrations step. Redwood and Helix walked through what “stuck at migrations” typically means (migrations job pending/crashlooping, database connectivity, schema lock, or resource constraints), align...
text: Helix Health reported a blocked Redwood Private upgrade where the installer progresses but hangs/fails at the database migrations step. Redwood and Helix walked through what “stuck at migrations” typically means (migrations job pending/crashlooping, database connectivity, schema lock, or resource constraints), aligned on a safe diagnostics bundle to collect, and agreed on a conservative rollback plan if forward-fix cannot be validated quickly. The conversation stayed focused on minimizing risk for a compliance-heavy environment and keeping Support/Engineering escalation paths clean (SUP-1845).

title: Northstar - Dedicated autoscaling oscillation incident review
preview: Post-incident review with Northstar on Dedicated autoscaling oscillation that caused intermittent latency spikes. Redwood walked through timeline and contributing factors (scale-in too aggressive + traffic spikiness + cold cache effects), confirmed interim mitigations already applied (min replicas raised, scale-down...
text: Post-incident review with Northstar on Dedicated autoscaling oscillation that caused intermittent latency spikes. Redwood walked through timeline and contributing factors (scale-in too aggressive + traffic spikiness + cold cache effects), confirmed interim mitigations already applied (min replicas raised, scale-down cooldown increased, scale-in dampening), and aligned on next configuration changes and monitoring. Ticket reference: SUP-1844. Follow-ups include Redwood guardrails work (ENG-4829), a short customer-facing write-up, and a joint load-test window to validate stability.

title: Northwind Analytics - POC check-in (performance + stability)
preview: Northwind shared results from the last 7 days of their POC load test. Median and p95 latencies improved vs their baseline, but they still see intermittent p99 spikes during bursty traffic and mixed prompt length distributions. Redwood linked the spikes to KV cache fragmentation behavior observed in canary and explai...
text: Northwind shared results from the last 7 days of their POC load test. Median and p95 latencies improved vs their baseline, but they still see intermittent p99 spikes during bursty traffic and mixed prompt length distributions. Redwood linked the spikes to KV cache fragmentation behavior observed in canary and explained the upcoming KV compaction + tier-aware eviction rollout plan and guardrails (budgets, metrics, kill-switch). Northwind confirmed remaining blockers are stability under bursts, clear rollback triggers, and a retest window once the canary is expanded. Agreed to rerun the benchmark after compaction canary reaches their hosted cohort and to align on success criteria for moving to Dedicated/VPC next.

title: Acme AI escalation: streaming timeouts / connection resets (SUP-1842)
preview: Acme reported intermittent streaming disconnects/timeouts in us-east causing truncated generations and retry storms in their proxy layer. Redwood confirmed elevated stream reset errors starting ~15:40 UTC and that the issue presents as mid-stream connection close with no final chunk. We agreed on a customer-side mit...
text: Acme reported intermittent streaming disconnects/timeouts in us-east causing truncated generations and retry storms in their proxy layer. Redwood confirmed elevated stream reset errors starting ~15:40 UTC and that the issue presents as mid-stream connection close with no final chunk. We agreed on a customer-side mitigation: (1) implement idempotent retry with jittered backoff and cap, (2) increase client/read timeout for streaming and ensure keepalive settings are sane, (3) temporarily disable HTTP/2 for the streaming route / force HTTP/1.1 where possible, and (4) log/capture request_id for every failed stream for faster correlation. Redwood will keep incident comms via email every 60 minutes while impact persists (and within 30 minutes for material changes), and will move to 2x/day updates once stabilized. Next steps: Redwood SRE to post an internal incident bridge update + share a s...

title: Demo feedback session - Sales Engineering (what broke + talk track tweaks)
preview: Internal SE/AE feedback on the new demo tenant + talk track. Main issues: (1) reset is still brittle/slow and sometimes leaves stale keys or missing seed objects, (2) dashboards are intermittently empty until synthetic traffic job is run, (3) 429 throttling shows up during live “economics” toggles / scripted runner ...
text: Internal SE/AE feedback on the new demo tenant + talk track. Main issues: (1) reset is still brittle/slow and sometimes leaves stale keys or missing seed objects, (2) dashboards are intermittently empty until synthetic traffic job is run, (3) 429 throttling shows up during live “economics” toggles / scripted runner bursts, (4) occasional RBAC mapping errors for SEs joining last-minute, (5) EU streaming disconnect workaround needs to be front-and-center. Messaging tweaks: don’t lead with “Optimize” as a product name; lead with “production economics controls” and show one concrete lever. Reliability/fallback story should be a shorter module unless prompted; keep it as an objection path but have a 60-second version ready. Team agreed on a tighter 30-min run-of-show and a ‘backup mode’ discipline (screenshots + canned outputs) when Console is flaky.

title: Aurora Devtools - technical deep dive: shadow evals + safe rollouts (Dedicated)
preview: Aurora Devtools (Dedicated prospect) requested a deep dive on Redwood’s rollout safety story with shadow traffic for quality metrics. Discussion covered: how shadow mirroring works (fail-open, strict timeouts), deterministic bucketing, streaming semantics, data retention/redaction controls, and which quality metrics...
text: Aurora Devtools (Dedicated prospect) requested a deep dive on Redwood’s rollout safety story with shadow traffic for quality metrics. Discussion covered: how shadow mirroring works (fail-open, strict timeouts), deterministic bucketing, streaming semantics, data retention/redaction controls, and which quality metrics Aurora needs to approve model/version/quant changes. Aurora wants quality gating alongside latency/cost during canaries, with an initial advisory mode and later optional auto-rollback after calibration. Redwood outlined v1 metric bundle (format adherence, refusal/safety, instruction following, rubric score) and statistical reporting (confidence intervals, minimum sample sizes).

title: Regional failover automation upgrade - kickoff
preview: Kickoff for the Reliability Program workstream to upgrade regional failover automation across Hosted API and Dedicated. Team aligned on problem statement (manual/partial automation, noisy triggers, slow detection, hard to validate), in-scope components (signal pipeline, region health aggregation, controller + determ...
text: Kickoff for the Reliability Program workstream to upgrade regional failover automation across Hosted API and Dedicated. Team aligned on problem statement (manual/partial automation, noisy triggers, slow detection, hard to validate), in-scope components (signal pipeline, region health aggregation, controller + deterministic policy evaluation, guardrails/rollback, observability, runbooks, game days) and out-of-scope items (multi-cloud migration, serving runtime rewrite, SLA language). Open questions focused on trigger hierarchy (authoritative signals vs corroboration), anti-flap behavior, approval model (auto vs ack-required), and how to safely run controlled production failovers with limited blast radius.

title: Benchmarking environment best practices (Redwood x NVIDIA)
preview: Redwood walked NVIDIA through the Q1 2026 perf baseline plan and asked for concrete guidance to reduce variance across baseline runs (H100/A100/L40S) and across regions. NVIDIA recommended standardizing on a small set of driver/CUDA combos, enabling persistence mode, avoiding mixed workloads on the same host, pinnin...
text: Redwood walked NVIDIA through the Q1 2026 perf baseline plan and asked for concrete guidance to reduce variance across baseline runs (H100/A100/L40S) and across regions. NVIDIA recommended standardizing on a small set of driver/CUDA combos, enabling persistence mode, avoiding mixed workloads on the same host, pinning CPU/NUMA, and using either application clocks or at minimum enforcing consistent power limits and monitoring GPU clocks/temps via DCGM. They also advised a consistent warmup phase and separating “cold” vs “warm cache” scenarios. Call ended with an agreed set of recommended settings and action items for Redwood to incorporate into the perf suite run manifest and benchmark fleet runbooks.

title: Northpeak AI - technical deep dive: tail latency under mixed short/long prompts
preview: Northpeak described user-visible p95 latency spikes when short chat requests are interleaved with long-context / RAG-heavy prompts (mixed workload). Current symptoms look like head-of-line blocking during prefill and unstable batching decisions. Redwood shared an in-flight scheduler prototype (short/long classificat...
text: Northpeak described user-visible p95 latency spikes when short chat requests are interleaved with long-context / RAG-heavy prompts (mixed workload). Current symptoms look like head-of-line blocking during prefill and unstable batching decisions. Redwood shared an in-flight scheduler prototype (short/long classification + two-queue dispatch with aging + per-iteration token budgets) aimed at reducing p95 without major throughput regression. Northpeak success criteria: materially improve p95/TTFT for short prompts under mixed load while guaranteeing progress for long prompts and keeping cost/throughput within bounds. Team agreed to run a joint benchmark using Redwood’s mixed-workload scenarios (80/20, 50/50, long-spike) and, if possible, replay a sanitized slice of Northpeak traces.

title: Post-launch retro - Benchmark report campaign (KPI review + refresh plan)
preview: Team reviewed performance of the public benchmark report launch (landing page + interactive table + PDF + blog + webinar). Results were strong top-of-funnel with meaningful sales-assisted pipeline influence, but launch-day reliability issues (cache miss storm + 429s) created support load and some negative chatter. T...
text: Team reviewed performance of the public benchmark report launch (landing page + interactive table + PDF + blog + webinar). Results were strong top-of-funnel with meaningful sales-assisted pipeline influence, but launch-day reliability issues (cache miss storm + 429s) created support load and some negative chatter. The methodology page and reproducibility artifacts were consistently cited as the trust driver. Next phase focuses on (1) tightening follow-up loops for high-intent leads, (2) shipping a lightweight “replicate this in your PoC” guide and a clearer metric explainer, (3) improving cache/CDN settings and guardrails for future spikes, and (4) establishing a refresh cadence with clear material-change thresholds and legal review gates.

title: Private upgrades technical deep dive (design partner) - upgrade plan, backups, rollback
preview: Northstar walked through current pain points upgrading their on-prem Redwood Private environment: upgrades are high-touch, require manual sequencing between control plane components and data plane, and rollback is mostly 'restore from VM snapshot and hope'. They emphasized air-gapped constraints (no outbound interne...
text:
Northstar walked through current pain points upgrading their on-prem Redwood Private environment: upgrades are high-touch, require manual sequencing between control plane components and data plane, and rollback is mostly 'restore from VM snapshot and hope'. They emphasized air-gapped constraints (no outbound internet), strict change windows, and audit evidence requirements.

Redwood reviewed the planned upgrade workflow (preflight checks -> plan generation -> backup -> staged rollout -> verification) and discussed which prechecks should block vs warn (storage headroom, DB connectivity, OIDC reachability). Backup approach was discussed across CSI snapshots and logical DB dumps, with Northstar noting they do not have consistent snapshot support across clusters.

Key concerns were around schema migrations (lock timeouts, partial upgrades), deterministic rollback hooks, and how artifacts ...

text: Northstar evaluated Redwood Hosted canary rollout workflow requirements for safely introducing new model versions/configs. Discussion focused on percentage traffic splitting, deterministic cohorting/stickiness, guardrails (max step size, soak time, approvals), auto-stop/rollback conditions based on SLO metrics, and audit logging/retention/SIEM export. Customer asked for evidence around change control (who approved what), and how rollouts interact with existing fallback policies and multi-region behavior.

text: Northstar platform/SRE/security team asked for concrete Runtime LTS commitments: 12-month support window expectations, monthly patch cadence + out-of-band security hotfixes, backport eligibility and SLA, performance regression gating and waiver handling, and how Redwood provides verifiable artifacts (checksums/signatures/provenance/SBOM) for Private deployments. Redwood outlined proposed runtime-1.18-lts branch model, risk rubric for backports, perf canary gates (p50/p95 latency + throughput), and artifact verification workflow tied to commits and release notes. Customer requested written language for vendor risk and a sample signed artifact + SBOM.

title: FinBank x Redwood — Private DR + backup/restore technical deep dive
preview: FinBank walked through DR requirements for a Redwood Private VPC deployment (plus a smaller, restricted environment with limited egress). Key asks: enterprise RPO/RTO targets, deterministic restore into a clean cluster, encryption with customer-managed keys, and evidence/validation of restores. Redwood described the...
text: FinBank walked through DR requirements for a Redwood Private VPC deployment (plus a smaller, restricted environment with limited egress). Key asks: enterprise RPO/RTO targets, deterministic restore into a clean cluster, encryption with customer-managed keys, and evidence/validation of restores. Redwood described the Day-2 toolkit direction: control-plane backups (manifest + encrypted artifact), optional scheduled backups via Helm CronJob, least-privilege S3+KMS patterns, and post-restore validation/smoke tests. Discussion covered whether Kubernetes resources are included (Redwood will back up Redwood control-plane/state + critical Helm values; Kubernetes/etcd snapshots are separate and only for cluster-level recovery), handling of secrets (references vs secret material), KMS/HSM constraints, and offline/air-gapped-ish restore workflows (signed bundles, no outbound calls, local checksu...
